title: Longest Palindromic Substring
description: Given a string s, return the longest palindromic substring in s. A substring is a contiguous sequence of characters within the string. A palindrome is a string that reads the same forward and backward.

examples.explanation: The input string is "babad". The longest palindromic substrings are "bab" and "aba", both of which have a length of 3. The output should return one of them.
In the string "cbbd", the longest palindromic substring is "bb", which has a length of 2. The characters 'c' and 'd' do not form a palindrome.
The input string is a single character "a". Any single character is a palindrome, so the output is "a".

constraints: The input string s has a length of at most 1000.
s consists of only digits and English letters.
hints: Consider checking for palindromes centered around each character and between each pair of characters.
Use a helper function to determine if a substring is a palindrome.
Keep track of the starting index and max length of the longest palindromic substring found.
Think about the difference between even-length and odd-length palindromes.
